action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 8 achieved a new best test EER of 12.41% (down from 17.92%) by adding telephone augmentations, attentive+mean pooling, and weighted sampling while keeping the encoder frozen. The training curves were empty because metrics.jsonl logging was missing. There remains a massive generalization gap (dev EER 0.87% vs test 12.41%), suggesting frozen WavLM features are not adapting to the unseen telephony codecs in the hidden test set. Analysis: The biggest remaining lever is fine-tuning the encoder (or at least the transformer layers) so representations adapt to codec-degraded speech. To preserve low-level stability, I will freeze only the CNN feature extractor and fine-tune the transformer + projection with a small LR (2e-5), while keeping a larger LR (1e-3) for the classifier head. I will also merge train+dev for more data, add explicit mu-law/a-law companding augmentations to simulate telephony codecs, and fix per-step metrics logging. Decision: Backup the previous best checkpoint, write a new train.py that implements these changes, run 2 epochs, verify predict.py on mock_test, and submit_eval.
action.command: cp checkpoints/best.pt checkpoints/best_frozen.pt && python train.py --epochs 2 --batch_size 32 --encoder_lr 2e-5 --head_lr 1e-3 && python predict.py --data_path mock_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output mock_test/pred.json
